title: Branch-less out-of-gas revert using returndatacopy with computed length
description: returndatacopy(0, 0, len) with len=0 is a no-op. With len=type(uint256).max it causes OOG. By computing len as sub(0, shr(N, value)), the length is 0 when value fits in N bits and wraps to max uint256 when it doesn't — achieving a conditional revert with zero branch opcodes.
pattern_after:
// No branch — the copy length itself is the condition
returndatacopy(returndatasize(), returndatasize(), sub(0, shr(48, number())))
when_to_apply: Uint range guards where saving a JUMPI opcode matters, typically in frequently called view functions
when_not_to_apply: When the revert reason needs to be distinguishable (OOG looks different to callers than a standard revert)
